reasoning: The CCC high yield index was at 11.92 on 2025-10-24, dropping from a recent peak of 12.36 mid-month. Despite this short-term downtrend, multiple qualitative factors suggest yields are structurally primed to rise or stabilize higher: spreads are currently near historical lows (4th percentile), leaving little room to tighten and asymmetrical risk for widening upon negative macro news. Furthermore, 10-year Treasury yields have stabilized near 4% despite the Fed's easing cycle, reducing downward pressure on base rates. Given the high daily volatility of the index (standard deviation ~0.14) and the mean reversion towards the ~12.25 recent average, a short-term bounce above 11.92 is favored. As per the strict instructions to increase uncertainty (meaning probabilities must decay toward 0.5) for longer horizons, the confidence of an increase is highest closely following the recent drop, and trends cleanly to 0.5 over 1- and 3-month horizons.
